name: Reynolds Number Calculator
description: Computes the Reynolds number for internal pipe flow to determine flow regime (laminar, transitional, turbulent) based on fluid properties and flow conditions. Compliant with ISO 5167, ASME MFC-2M, and Six Sigma DMAIC methodology.

inputs.label: Fluid Type
inputs.businessContext: Select the working fluid to auto-populate density and viscosity. Options include water, air, oil, and custom.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select the working fluid to auto-populate density and viscosity. Options include water, air, oil, and custom.

inputs.label: Fluid Density
inputs.businessContext: Mass density of the fluid at operating temperature. For water at 20°C: 998.2 kg/m³.

businessContext_i18n.en: Mass density of the fluid at operating temperature. For water at 20°C: 998.2 kg/m³.

inputs.label: Dynamic Viscosity
inputs.businessContext: Absolute viscosity of the fluid. For water at 20°C: 0.001002 Pa·s.

businessContext_i18n.en: Absolute viscosity of the fluid. For water at 20°C: 0.001002 Pa·s.

inputs.label: Flow Velocity
inputs.businessContext: Average velocity of the fluid in the pipe cross-section.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average velocity of the fluid in the pipe cross-section.

inputs.label: Pipe Inner Diameter
inputs.businessContext: Internal diameter of the pipe or conduit.

businessContext_i18n.en: Internal diameter of the pipe or conduit.

inputs.label: Pipe Roughness
inputs.businessContext: Average height of surface irregularities inside the pipe. For commercial steel: 0.000045 m.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average height of surface irregularities inside the pipe. For commercial steel: 0.000045 m.

inputs.label: Operating Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Fluid temperature used for viscosity and density corrections if applicable.

businessContext_i18n.en: Fluid temperature used for viscosity and density corrections if applicable.

inputs.label: Flow Regime Threshold
inputs.businessContext: Select threshold standard: 'standard' (Re<2300 laminar, 2300-4000 transitional, >4000 turbulent) or 'conservative' (Re<2000 laminar, 2000-3000 transitional, >3000 turbulent).

businessContext_i18n.en: Select threshold standard: 'standard' (Re<2300 laminar, 2300-4000 transitional, >4000 turbulent) or 'conservative' (Re<2000 laminar, 2000-3000 transitional, >3000 turbulent).

rules.message: Dynamic viscosity must be greater than zero.
Density must be greater than zero.
Temperature outside recommended range (-50°C to 500°C). Results may be inaccurate.

thresholds.message: Flow is laminar. Low mixing, high viscous forces dominate.
Flow is transitional. Unstable regime; design for either laminar or turbulent.
Flow is turbulent. High mixing, increased friction losses.
Fully turbulent rough regime. Friction factor becomes independent of Re (Moody chart zone).
Very low velocity may cause sedimentation or stratification.

formulas.name: Kinematic Viscosity
formulas.description: Ratio of dynamic viscosity to density.

formulas.name: Relative Roughness
formulas.description: Ratio of pipe roughness to diameter.

formulas.name: Darcy Friction Factor (Colebrook-White)
formulas.description: Iterative solution for turbulent flow friction factor.

formulas.name: Pressure Drop per Meter
formulas.description: Head loss per unit length using Darcy-Weisbach equation.

formulas.name: Flow Regime Classification
formulas.description: Determines laminar, transitional, or turbulent based on Re and threshold standard.
formulas.equation: if Re < Re_low then 'Laminar' else if Re <= Re_high then 'Transitional' else 'Turbulent'

formulas.name: Mass Flow Rate
formulas.description: Mass of fluid flowing per unit time.
formulas.equation: ṁ = ρ * A * V, where A = π * D² / 4

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Friction Factor
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Friction factor > 0.03 indicates excessive energy loss due to pipe roughness or small diameter.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Transitional Flow Instability
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Flow in transitional regime causes unpredictable pressure drops and potential vibration.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Velocity Sedimentation Risk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Velocity below 0.5 m/s may allow solids to settle in the pipe.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Pressure Drop
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Pressure drop > 500 Pa/m indicates significant pumping energy waste.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Pipe Diameter
suggestedActions.description: If Reynolds number is too high or pressure drop excessive, consider a larger pipe diameter to reduce velocity and friction.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Pipe Roughness
suggestedActions.description: If friction factor is high, use smoother pipe material (e.g., plastic or lined steel) to lower energy losses.

suggestedActions.label: Adjust Flow Rate
suggestedActions.description: If flow is transitional, increase or decrease velocity to achieve stable laminar or turbulent regime.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Velocity
suggestedActions.description: If sedimentation risk is high, increase flow velocity above 0.5 m/s to keep solids suspended.

suggestedActions.label: Use Viscosity Reducer
suggestedActions.description: If Reynolds number is too low (laminar) and mixing is needed, consider heating or adding a viscosity reducer.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Reynolds number adjusted for input uncertainty using Monte Carlo simulation (assumes ±5% variation in velocity, diameter, viscosity).
